---
title: "Case-control Analysis"
author: "Shuyi Ji"
date: "2026-03-19"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(MatchIt)
library(survival)
library(dplyr)
library(pbapply)
library(parallel)

target_data <- Vacc2year

# List of 11 pathogens to iterate through
pathogens_list <- c("InfA", "InfB", "HPIV", "HADV", "Ch", "HRV", "HMPV", "HCOV", "HRSV", "Mp", "Boca")

# Fixed baseline matching covariates
base_covariates <- c("Gender_num", "Age_years", "Month_num", "Type")

# Define the analysis function for Bootstrap/Iterations
analyze_once <- function(i, pathogen, formula_str, exact_formula_str) {
  # Shuffle data order to ensure randomness in nearest neighbor matching
  shuffled_data <- target_data[sample(nrow(target_data)), ]
  
  # Perform matching
  matched_data <- matchit(
    as.formula(formula_str),
    data = shuffled_data,
    method = "nearest",
    distance = "glm",
    ratio = 1,
    exact = as.formula(exact_formula_str),
    discard = "both",
    replace = FALSE,
    caliper = 0.01
  )

  # Extract matched dataset
  matched_df <- match.data(matched_data)

  # Return NA if matching fails to find any pairs
  if (nrow(matched_df) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      Iteration = i, Pathogen = pathogen, OR = NA, Lower_CI = NA, Upper_CI = NA, 
      P_value = NA, N_cases = NA, N_controls = NA
    ))
  }

  # Retain strict 1:1 subclasses (one case and one control per subclass)
  matched_df <- matched_df %>%
    group_by(subclass) %>%
    filter(n() == 2, sum(!!sym(pathogen)) == 1) %>% 
    ungroup()

  # Re-check after filtering subclasses
  if (nrow(matched_df) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      Iteration = i, Pathogen = pathogen, OR = NA, Lower_CI = NA, Upper_CI = NA, 
      P_value = NA, N_cases = NA, N_controls = NA
    ))
  }

  # Conditional Logistic Regression
  result_df <- tryCatch({
    model <- clogit(
      as.formula(paste(pathogen, "~ Pre_vaccination + strata(subclass)")),
      data = matched_df
    )
    
    # Extract results
    OR <- exp(coef(model))
    OR_CI <- exp(confint(model))
    p_val <- summary(model)$coefficients["Pre_vaccination", "Pr(>|z|)"]

    data.frame(
      Iteration = i, 
      Pathogen = pathogen, 
      OR = OR, 
      Lower_CI = OR_CI[1], 
      Upper_CI = OR_CI[2], 
      P_value = p_val,
      N_cases = sum(matched_df[[pathogen]] == 1), 
      N_controls = sum(matched_df[[pathogen]] == 0)
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    data.frame(
      Iteration = i, Pathogen = pathogen, OR = NA, Lower_CI = NA, Upper_CI = NA, 
      P_value = NA, N_cases = sum(matched_df[[pathogen]] == 1), N_controls = sum(matched_df[[pathogen]] == 0)
    )
  })

  return(result_df)
}

# 3. Start Parallel Computing
n_cores <- floor(detectCores() / 2)
message("Starting parallel computation using core count: ", n_cores)

cl <- makeCluster(n_cores)

# Load required packages on each worker node
clusterEvalQ(cl, {
  library(MatchIt)
  library(survival)
  library(dplyr)
})

# Distribute external data (target_data) and function to worker nodes
clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("target_data", "analyze_once"))

# Loop through the 11 pathogens
all_results <- list()

for (pathogen in pathogens_list) {
  message("\n==================================")
  message("Processing pathogen: ", pathogen)
  
  # Identify the other 10 pathogens to be used as covariates
  other_pathogens <- setdiff(pathogens_list, pathogen)
  
  # Matching formula: base variables + remaining 10 pathogens
  matching_covariates <- c(base_covariates, other_pathogens)
  
  # Pre-construct formula strings for matchit
  formula_str <- paste(pathogen, "~", paste(matching_covariates, collapse = " + "))
  exact_formula_str <- paste("~", paste(matching_covariates, collapse = " + "))
  
  message("Matching criteria includes: ", length(matching_covariates), " variables")
  
  # Export current pathogen-specific formulas to individual cores
  clusterExport(cl, varlist = c("pathogen", "formula_str", "exact_formula_str"), envir = environment())
  
  # Run 1000 iterations via Parallel Bootstrap
  results <- pblapply(1:1000, function(i) analyze_once(i, pathogen, formula_str, exact_formula_str), cl = cl)
  
  # Combine the 1000 results for the current pathogen
  all_results[[pathogen]] <- do.call(rbind, results)
}

# Close cluster and release resources
stopCluster(cl)
message("\nParallel computation completed")

# Generate final detailed results
final_results_detail <- do.call(rbind, all_results)
```
